title: astragalus lentiginosus var. ursinus
intro: Astragalus lentiginosus var. ursinus, also known as  mountain milkvetch, is a perennial legume native to the western United States and Canada. It has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic properties, which enable the body to resist stress and promote overall well-being. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, making it beneficial for health issues such as fatigue, cardiovascular disease, and respiratory problems. Astragalus lentiginosus var. ursinus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
